latestEntry.notes: Week 6 Day 6 of Talk 4 (Week 2 of 2). Case Study focus: The 5 helpful notions as diagnostic tools for pattern recognition. Core frameworks: 5 Notions as Diagnostic Tools, Lollapalooza Detection, Violation Pattern Recognition. 5 case snapshots - 2008 Financial Crisis (multiple violations: no simplification, weak numerical fluency, no inversion, ignored elementary wisdom, missed lollapalooza risk), TikTok's explosive growth (applied lollapalooza: combined algorithmic personalization, social proof, variable rewards, dopamine loops, low-friction), startup founder who optimized for wrong metric (violated simplification by not asking 'which customers should we retain?'), Berkshire's GEICO acquisition (applied all 5 notions: simplification, numerical fluency, inversion, elementary wisdom, lollapalooza), Quibi's $1.75B failure (ignored simplification, inversion, and elementary wisdom). Key takeaway: Run every major decision through the full 5-notion checklist, build violation pattern library, look for 3+ factor alignments, practice mental simulation, use case studies as training data. Commitment: Pick one real-world situation and run it through the full 5-notion checklist. Message delivered successfully.
